title: Stable Cursor Timelines, Filters, Reverse Navigation, and Reflow · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for stable cursor timelines, filters, reverse navigation, and reflow and accepts only runtime and compiler evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, Node, network, XML, PostgreSQL, handle, load, accessibility, deployment, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for stable accessible entry timeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Choose a documented total order with unique tie-breaker and state which edits can move an entry.
Runtime-validate version, tenant, direction, sort values, filters, expiry, and integrity without granting authority or query control.
Implement forward and reverse keyset predicates aligned with sort, null policy, index, limit-plus-one, and output reversal.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Feed Ts Pagination Reader Ux
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz TypeScript team handles case 9272 during a cross-tenant privacy and cache audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove deterministic ordering, signed cursors, keyset directions, changing data, and accessible navigation; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant work must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one tenant, cursor, reader command, authorization, validator, sanitizer, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Provide keyboard-operable next and previous navigation, focus continuity, result context, URL state, reflow, and no infinite-scroll dependency.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?
